title: Quantitative Bloch Decay for Spin-1/2 Nuclei
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2s1cg","text":"Purpose ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"139i7","text":"To acquire a 1D MAS spectrum of a spin-1/2 nucleus with quantitative intensities. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4kdef","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jn93","text":"Scope ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"et499","text":"The acquisition of 1D NMR spectra of spin-1/2 nuclei with quantitative relative intensities requires attention to be paid to the T1 relaxation of each resonance. This SOP should only be followed when quantitative relative intensities are required. If only lineshapes or chemical shifts are required, the d1 relaxation period can be reduced in duration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":129,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":130,"length":1},{"style":"bold","offset":304,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ejvke","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ftkjv","text":"If there is a significant rotor or probe background, an echo sequence may be more appropriate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vuuu","text":"The quantitative intensities described here are relative intensities, not absolute intensities. Determining absolute intensities, sometimes known as spin counting, is outside the scope of this SOP. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5t0jk","text":"User should be familiar with the power limitations and duty cycle of the probe being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d0k6c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7g56j","text":"User should be familiar with the maximum safe spinning speed of the probe and rotor being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fudtu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9qdkm","text":"Accurate T1 relaxation times and calibrated pulse lengths must have been obtained prior to starting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":9,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":10,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d382g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5vk32","text":"The expected amount of time to completion is highly sample-dependent and cannot be accurately estimated ahead of time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"19j6h","text":"High-power decoupling can cause damage to a probe if applied incorrectly. Ensure that the decoupling power and duration are set within safe limits.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Set the relaxation time d1 to 5 × T 1 , where T 1 is the largest value previously measured.
Set the pulse length p1 and pulse power plw1 to a previously optimized 90° pulse.
Set the 1 H decoupling program, CPDPRG2 , pulse length, PCPD2 , and pulse power, plw12 , to a previously optimized 1 H decoupling condition.
If no 1 H decoupling is required, set the pulse power plw12 to 0 W.

Set the acquisition time aq to a sufficiently long value such that the FID is not truncated, but less than 50 ms if decoupling is used. Applying high-power decoupling for more than 50 ms can cause physical damage to the probe. Ensure that the relaxation time d1 is long enough to respect the duty cycle of the probe. This typically requires d1 to be at least 20x longer than aq .

Adjust ns to effectively make use of available experimental time while achieving sufficient signal-to-noise ratios. If a good number for ns cannot be estimated, spectra can be acquired in blocks and added via fidsum .
Process the spectrum while paying careful attention to background subtraction and baseline correction if necessary.
Obtain the relative intensities of the resonances via integration if well-resolved, or deconvolution if not.
creator: NMRFAM Facility
